summary:
The stain not typically used in tattoos among those listed is \(\textbf{others}\), specifically excluding A) Osimum Blue as it’s primarily an electron microscope staining reagent rather than a component involved with actual ink deposition during tattoo creation. To be precise though based strictly from provided options only 'osmium' stands out due its unique role unrelated directly to visible pigmentation techniques employed by artists when creating permanent skin art pieces through injection methods utilizing dyes/inks designed explicitly for this purpose without any direct relevance towards lipid labeling via EM technique performed post mortem using specialized fixatives including osmium tetroxide solutions amongst others but never intended nor applied onto living human epidermis layers where most common commercial grade pigment suspensions reside after being injected into dermal layer beneath surface keratinocytes thus making option B correct if restated accurately reflecting true intent behind original query posed regarding The stain uniquely unsuitable for use in traditional tattooing procedures compared against other candidates outlined herein would logically have no place there fundamentally because unlike
